mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals expanding household demand and near-term revenue momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS growth is stronger than its recent average.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value is 505826.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 forward returns, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate tightens discount rates and financial conditions, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after a clear tightening impulse.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.50pp over 3 months or crosses above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value is 2.4 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals deteriorating income growth and recession risk, leading investors to mark down expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.30pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value is 4.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day S&P 500 forward returns after triggered UNRATE increases are not lower than unconditional returns at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion accompanied by a flat or falling unemployment rate signals broad labor demand without slack deterioration, supporting consumption and earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value is 150574.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-nonrising signal has no incremental explanatory power over either series alone in walk-forward 20-day return tests.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production with rising consumer prices identifies a stagflation shock that compresses margins and keeps policy restrictive, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change < 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value is 109.4447 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered stagflation episodes do not have lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than episodes with only weak INDPRO or only high CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and slower future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% year over year
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value is 1256.0 as of t-3mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST year-over-year declines greater than 10% are not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth indicates an expanding aggregate demand base that supports corporate revenues and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when measured as an always-on growth trend.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value is 18664.973 as of t-7mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth states fail to produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative GDPC1 growth states across two consecutive walk-forward evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Disinflation combined with a stable or falling policy rate lowers expected discount rates without signaling immediate policy shock, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change falls by more than 1pp and FEDFUNDS 3-month change <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value is 252.673 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint disinflation and nonrising FEDFUNDS condition does not improve 5-day S&P 500 return hit rate relative to unconditional returns by at least 5 percentage points.
